title: Provider
objectives: Explain Provider as an InheritedWidget wrapper

sections.markdown: Provider is a lightweight wrapper around InheritedWidget that makes dependency injection and state propagation simple. It exposes objects down the tree and rebuilds listeners when they change.
Calling context.watch in callbacks (rebuild loops) or wrapping a huge subtree in Consumer so everything rebuilds. Use read() in callbacks and Selector for granular rebuilds.
Provider is great for small-to-medium apps and is the official baseline. For larger apps many teams move to Riverpod (its spiritual successor) for compile-time safety.

flashcards.back: InheritedWidget — it wraps it to simplify DI and state propagation.
watch() subscribes and rebuilds on change; read() is a one-off access (use in callbacks).
Use Selector<T,S> so only widgets depending on S rebuild.

quiz.q: Provider is essentially a wrapper around…
quiz.explain: Provider simplifies InheritedWidget for DI + state.

quiz.q: In a button callback you should use…

quiz.explain: Selector rebuilds only when the selected value changes.

challenges.text: Build a counter with ChangeNotifier + Provider.
Use Selector to rebuild only one label of many.
Compose multiple providers and a ProxyProvider dependency.
Profile and eliminate over-rebuilds in a Provider-based list screen.

interviewQuestions: What is Provider and what is it built on?
How does Provider avoid prop drilling?
